cases.note: deep nest (ARCHITECTURE.md В§10): nested simple-fraction NUMERATOR over letter вҖ” the inner a/b has no fence, so the outer takes compound form в ҶвҖҰв °: open, a/b, blank, bar, c, close

cases.note: deep nest: nested simple-fraction DENOMINATOR вҖ” compound form в ҶвҖҰв °: open, a, blank, bar, b/c, close. Distinct from the numerator-nested case above (blank sits after a, not after a/b), so (a/b)/c and a/(b/c) are no longer ambiguous

cases.note: deep nest: triple nesting. Innermost a/b is simple вҶ’ middle (a/b)/c is compound в ҶвҖҰв ° (self-fenced). The outer numerator is now a fenced compound fraction, so the outer is back to simple form: compound(a/b/c) bar d
